fields.description:
## Why
The reconcile-bridge outage (bug 8002-a92f-0999-45dd) was an *unparseable* workflow:
`f2bde8d3b5` put `${{ runner.temp }}` in a job-level `env:` of `reconcile-bridge.yml` (and
its canary) â€” a GitHub Actions startup failure (0 jobs) that stopped Jira sync for ~2 days.
**CI never caught it** because `make lint` (the gate the Gerrit `Verified` vote runs) scopes
actionlint to `release.yml` ONLY (`Makefile`, `RELEASE_WORKFLOW`; comment line 101: "Scoped
to release.yml â€” NOT repo-wide"). Every other workflow â€” including the bridge + canary â€” is
unvalidated, so an invalid-workflow change merges green and silently disables the workflow.

## What
Widen the CI **actionlint** coverage in `make lint` from `release.yml` to **all**
`.github/workflows/*.{yml,yaml}`, so a parse-error / bad-context change is blocked at the
`Verified` gate. Cost is zero today: `actionlint .github/workflows/*.yml
.github/workflows/*.yaml` already exits 0 with 0 findings (post-8002-fix). **zizmor stays
scoped to `release.yml`** â€” widening the security audit is a separate concern, out of scope.

## Scope
- `Makefile` lint target: invoke actionlint over ALL workflows (actionlint auto-discovers
  `.github/workflows` when given no path arg), keeping `zizmor $(RELEASE_WORKFLOW)` unchanged
  and preserving the macOS-skip + pinned-binary install logic. Update the actionlint-scope
  comments (lines ~19, 97, 101, 107, 115) that say "release.yml only"/"NOT repo-wide" so they
  describe the widened actionlint scope (noting zizmor remains release.yml-scoped).
- `docs/releasing.md:129`: correct "zizmor + actionlint scoped to `release.yml`" â†’
  "zizmor scoped to `release.yml`; actionlint covers all workflows".
- `tests/unit/test_ci_actionlint_scope.py` (new): assert the Makefile lint recipe runs
  actionlint over all workflows (not `$(RELEASE_WORKFLOW)`-only), guarding a silent re-narrow.

## How it is guarded (accurate â€” corrects the prior plan)
Both `gerrit-verify.yaml:303` (pre-merge Verified) and `test.yml:55` (post-merge branch CI)
run `make lint`, so widening the Makefile target covers both legs automatically; a
reintroduced bad workflow fails that `make lint` step. NOTE: the existing
`tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py` only asserts both legs invoke the *string* `make lint`
â€” it does NOT verify actionlint's scope, so it is NOT the guard for this change. The new
`test_ci_actionlint_scope.py` is that guard.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `make lint` runs actionlint against every `.github/workflows/*.yml`/`*.yaml`, not just
  `release.yml`. Proof: `sed -n '/^lint:/,/^actionlint-bin:/p' Makefile` shows the actionlint
  invocation is not restricted to `$(RELEASE_WORKFLOW)`.
- [ ] `make lint` passes on the current tree. Proof: `make lint` exits 0.
- [ ] Reintroducing a `${{ runner.temp }}` job-level env into any workflow makes `make lint`
  FAIL for the runner-context reason. Proof: add the line, run `make lint` â†’ non-zero exit
  citing `runner`, then `git checkout` to revert.
- [ ] A regression test asserts the Makefile actionlints all workflows and fails if re-scoped
  to `release.yml` only. Proof: `.venv/bin/pytest tests/unit/test_ci_actionlint_scope.py -q`.
- [ ] No stale "actionlint scoped to release.yml" wording remains. Proof:
  `grep -rniE 'actionlint[^.]*scoped to .?release\.yml|actionlint[^.]*release\.yml only' Makefile docs/`
  returns nothing (accurate zizmor-scoped references may remain).
